trade.summary: Corea del Sud-Paesi Bassi trade is critically shaped by ASML's dominant position in semiconductor lithography — Samsung and SK Hynix are among ASML's largest customers, purchasing EUV and DUV machines for their advanced chip fabs in Corea del Sud. This makes the Paesi Bassi one of Corea del Sud's most strategically important technology partners. Both countries benefit from the EU-Corea del Sud FTA (2011).
top_products_a_to_b: semiconductors and memory chips (Samsung, SK Hynix)
disputes: Corea del Sudn chipmakers affected by Dutch export restrictions on ASML tools to Cina, though Corea del Sud itself is an approved buyer

diplomatic.summary: Corea del Sud-Paesi Bassi relations are strong and increasingly strategically significant due to the semiconductor technology nexus. Both are liberal democracies and members of multilateral economic institutions. They cooperate on technology export controls, climate policy, and trade. The EU-Corea del Sud FTA provides a solid legal framework. Paesi Bassi is an important EU partner for Corea del Sud.

military.summary: Both countries are Stati Uniti security partners. The Paesi Bassi participated in the Corea del Sudn War under UN command (a point of historical connection). Growing alignment on technology security. No formal bilateral defense treaty. Both countries support Ucraina against Russia.
military.alliance_status: Aligned — Netherlands NATO, Korea US ally

financial.summary: Significant bilateral investment driven by semiconductor equipment and technology. Samsung and SK Hynix have purchasing and collaborative R&D relationships with ASML. Dutch institutional investors hold Corea del Sudn equities. Corea del Sudn conglomerates have European headquarters considerations in the Paesi Bassi due to its favorable tax and legal environment.

energy.summary: Limited direct bilateral energy trade. Shell (Paesi Bassi) has some LNG supply relationships with Corea del Sudn buyers. Both countries cooperate on hydrogen and offshore wind technology.

scientific.summary: Strong science and technology cooperation centered on semiconductors. Samsung, SK Hynix, and Corea del Sudn research institutes have joint R&D with ASML and Dutch research institutions. Cooperation in photonics, nanotechnology, and materials science is substantial. Both countries are significant R&D spenders.
